---
title: "LRP52d: Period-1 ITT (W) with dose — group τ vs dose restatement"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
```

## Overview

LRP52d is the [#104](https://github.com/dseinternational/language-reading-predictors/issues/104)
Phase-2 **ITT restatement**. It is the [LRP52](../lrp52/) period-1 (t1$\to$t2)
ITT model for word reading ($W$) with the **period-1 dose** (`attend` at t1) added
as a single linear adjuster alongside the randomised group term, via `fit_itt` +
`extra["adjust_for"]` — no new machinery. The period-1 ITT is already identified
by randomisation regardless of pooling, so the narrow new value is to make the
**dose-absorbs-group** finding from the Phase-1 GB diagnostic (Q3) explicit.

## How to read it — group τ vs dose

- **$\tau$ is the randomised, primary causal estimand**, the coefficient on $G$.
  **$G$ is coded $G = 1$ = waitlist control, $G = 0$ = immediate intervention**
  (`preprocessing.py` maps dataset group 2 [Wait] $\to 1$, group 1 $\to 0$; its
  inline comment is inverted). So a **negative $\tau$ means the immediate arm
  scores higher** (treatment benefit), and `prob_tau_pos` = $P(\tau > 0)$ is
  $P(\text{waitlist higher})$, **not** $P(\text{treatment helps})$. Interpret the
  sign with the arm labels, not the bare probability.
- **`gamma_attend` is the period-1 dose slope** (per 1 SD of sessions). In period
  1 `attend` and $G$ are near-collinear — the waitlist controls all have
  `attend == 0` — so entering both **splits the one randomised contrast between
  them**: the continuous dose carries the signal the binary indicator otherwise
  would. Report the two **side by side** (the mechanism, dose, vs the estimand,
  assignment), not as independent effects. Compare $\tau$ here against $\tau$ in
  LRP52 (group only) to see how much the dose term absorbs.

**Caveat.** The period-1 dose is observational *within* the randomised
assignment; this is an interpretive restatement of the randomised effect, not a
second causal estimand.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=40%}
![tau](prior_tau.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_cross](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=40%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=40%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

`gamma_attend` (the period-1 dose slope) is in the table above.

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}
![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Treatment-effect summary (τ)

```{python}
# | echo: false
tau_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "tau_summary.csv")
tau_summary
```

- `tau_logit_*`: posterior mean and equal-tailed quantiles of $\tau$ on the logit
  scale (coefficient on $G$, $G = 1$ = waitlist control).
- `tau_prob_*`: implied average marginal effect on the probability scale.
- `prob_tau_pos`: $P(\tau > 0)$ = $P(\text{waitlist higher})$ — see the coding
  note above.

## Posterior predictive

![Posterior predictive check](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
